mew-archivist.name: commit-suggest
mew-archivist.description: Generate a conventional commit message from session activity

mew-archivist.name: doc-coauthoring
mew-archivist.description: Guide users through a structured 3-stage workflow for co-authoring any substantial document — session summaries, reports, specs, or decision docs. Use when the user wants structured writing help beyond a simple log entry.

mew-archivist.name: internal-comms
mew-archivist.description: Write internal communications — status reports, project updates, 3P updates (Progress/Plans/Problems), incident reports, FAQs, session wraps. Use whenever the user needs structured written communication about project state.

mew-archivist.name: log-write
mew-archivist.description: Write a single log entry to a project's log.md

mew-archivist.name: notify
mew-archivist.description: Send notifications via Telegram or Slack. Use at session wrap, when a milestone is hit, or when a blocker is found. Handles message formatting and API delivery.

mew-archivist.name: session-wrap
mew-archivist.description: Full session-end sequence — log, status, sync, commit suggestion

mew-chief.name: dispatcher
mew-chief.description: Classify every request and route to the right sub-agent

mew-chief.name: schedule
mew-chief.description: Schedule recurring or one-shot prompts using CronCreate. Use for session wrap reminders, daily standups, recurring research checks, or any timed task. Durable jobs persist across Claude restarts.

mew-coder.name: claude-api
mew-coder.description: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Use when code imports anthropic or @anthropic-ai/sdk, or when adding/tuning any Claude feature (caching, tool use, streaming, batch). Skip for OpenAI or other providers.

mew-coder.name: code-review
mew-coder.description: Security and quality review of changed files

mew-coder.name: github-issue-fix
mew-coder.description: 6-step pipeline to triage, fix, review, and PR a GitHub issue

mew-coder.name: mcp-builder
mew-coder.description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that connect external services to Claude. Use when building or extending any MCP server in mewvault/ or software-projects/.

mew-coder.name: refactor
mew-coder.description: Safe refactor with verification gate — no behaviour changes

mew-coder.name: tdd-workflow
mew-coder.description: Test-first implementation cycle for any new src/ or lib/ file

mew-coder.name: webapp-testing
mew-coder.description: Test local web applications using Playwright — verify frontend functionality, debug UI behavior, capture screenshots, view browser logs. Use when testing any web app in software-projects/ or when the user wants to verify UI behaviour.

mew-coder.name: xlsx
mew-coder.description: Create, read, edit, or fix .xlsx/.csv/.tsv files. Use whenever a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output — adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, cleaning data, financial models. Trigger when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path, even casually.

mew-designer.name: canvas-design
mew-designer.description: Create original visual art as .png or .pdf — posters, abstract compositions, editorial graphics. Two-step workflow: design philosophy then canvas execution. Use when the user wants a designed visual artifact, not a wireframe or UI component.

mew-designer.name: figma-spec
mew-designer.description: Extract component specs from Figma via MCP and produce handoff notes

mew-designer.name: frontend-design
mew-designer.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use when building web components, pages, or apps that need polished styling — even if the user doesn't say "design" explicitly.

mew-designer.name: image-gen
mew-designer.description: Generate images via AI APIs (FAL/Flux, Stability AI, DALL-E 3). Use when the user wants an AI-generated image as a design asset — concept art, mockup backgrounds, icons, textures. Handles prompt engineering, API call, and saving to assets/.

mew-designer.name: theme-factory
mew-designer.description: Apply consistent professional themes (colors + fonts) to any artifact — slides, docs, HTML pages, reports. Has 10 preset themes or can generate a custom one on request.

mew-designer.name: token-audit
mew-designer.description: Audit design token usage — missing aliases, unused tokens, coverage gaps

mew-designer.name: web-artifacts-builder
mew-designer.description: Build elaborate multi-component React artifacts using React 18 + Tailwind + shadcn/ui. Use for complex UIs requiring state management, routing, or shadcn components — not for simple single-file HTML.

mew-gamedev.name: algorithmic-art
mew-gamedev.description: Create algorithmic/generative art using p5.js with seeded randomness and interactive parameter controls. Two-step workflow: algorithmic philosophy then interactive HTML artifact. Use when the user wants living, code-driven visual art — not static design.

mew-gamedev.name: frontend-design
mew-gamedev.description: Create production-grade UI for game menus, HUDs, and overlays. Use when designing any visual interface for a game project in game-lab/.

mew-gamedev.name: gdscript-review
mew-gamedev.description: Review GDScript for Godot conventions and performance issues

mew-gamedev.name: godot-coder
mew-gamedev.description: Write production-ready GDScript for Godot 4 mechanics — character movement, physics, signals, state machines, UI.

mew-gamedev.name: godot-debug
mew-gamedev.description: Troubleshoot Godot runtime errors and GDScript bugs from pasted error output.

mew-gamedev.name: godot-session-start
mew-gamedev.description: Session-start ritual for Godot projects — reads project state and proposes the next mechanic to build.

mew-gamedev.name: mechanic-design
mew-gamedev.description: Design a game mechanic with feel notes and Godot implementation sketch

mew-gamedev.name: webapp-testing
mew-gamedev.description: Test web-based game builds using Playwright — verify game UI, browser rendering, and interactive behaviour. Use for game-lab projects with a web frontend or browser-based prototype.

mew-ideator.name: idea-brief
mew-ideator.description: Produce a one-page pitch summary (brief.md) from an idea folder. Use when the user wants to present, share, or pitch an idea — even if they just say "summarise this idea" or "give me a one-pager for X".

mew-ideator.name: idea-capture
mew-ideator.description: Take a rough prompt or brain dump and scaffold a structured idea.md in idea-hub/ideas/<slug>/. Use whenever the user describes a new idea, mentions wanting to capture something, or dumps raw thoughts — even if they don't say "capture idea" explicitly.

mew-ideator.name: idea-expand
mew-ideator.description: Deepen a seed idea by surfacing the real problem, specific user pain, pivot angles, analogous products, and moats. Modifies idea.md in-place. Use when an idea is captured but thin — user wants to think it through before validating.

mew-ideator.name: pptx
mew-ideator.description: Create, edit, or extract content from .pptx presentations. Use whenever the user mentions deck, slides, presentation, or a .pptx file — even if pptx isn't the final goal. For idea-hub, this produces pitch decks from idea briefs.

mew-learner.name: concept-distill
mew-learner.description: Distil a source document into structured concept pages

mew-learner.name: ingest-pdf
mew-learner.description: Process a raw PDF or document into wiki concept pages

mew-learner.name: pdf
mew-learner.description: Use whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files — read, extract text/tables, merge, split, rotate, create, OCR scanned pages. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.

mew-planner.name: doc-coauthoring
mew-planner.description: Guide users through a structured 3-stage workflow for co-authoring documentation — proposals, specs, MewKing plans, decision docs. Use when the user wants to write any substantial structured document.

mew-planner.name: risk-map
mew-planner.description: Surface risks and mitigations before implementation begins

mew-planner.name: tier-analysis
mew-planner.description: Classify a task as Pounce / Stalk / MewKing with reasoning

mew-planner.name: write-mewking-plan
mew-planner.description: Produce a MewKing plan.md with phases, files, risks, and acceptance tests

mew-researcher.name: browser-use
mew-researcher.description: Systematic multi-page web research — traverse paginated results, extract structured data, track sources. Use when WebSearch alone isn't enough and you need to read full pages, follow links, or extract data across multiple URLs.

mew-researcher.name: competitor-map
mew-researcher.description: Structured competitive landscape for an idea or market — positioning, gaps, entry angle. Use whenever the user wants to understand who already plays in a space, even outside the idea-hub lifecycle.

mew-researcher.name: effort-estimate
mew-researcher.description: T-shirt size an idea or feature — S/M/L/XL, phase count, stack recommendation, solo-viable flag. Use when the user wants to know how much work something is before committing.

mew-researcher.name: feasibility-scan
mew-researcher.description: Research and assess an idea's market size, competitors, tech complexity, and effort. Writes feasibility.md with a green/amber/red verdict. Use when the user wants to know if an idea is worth building — even if they just ask "is this viable?" or "should I build this?".

mew-researcher.name: xlsx
mew-researcher.description: Create, read, edit, or fix .xlsx/.csv/.tsv files. Use whenever a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output — market sizing tables, competitor comparison grids, feasibility data, research exports. Trigger when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path, even casually.
